ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 650–1000L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS8-L1-6501000-MSELA8-HMX-IFN-01 | MS ELA: Grade 8 | Narrative Techniques (Dialogue, Pacing, Description, Reflection) | Haunted Manuscript Exchange | Indigenous & First Nations Knowledge
Voices in the Margins
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The girl is discussing her opinion about an old manuscript they found. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the girl gives to support her opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1:
Why does the girl bring up First Nations storytellers?
2 QUESTION 2:
Where did the girl learn that storytellers passed stories down by telling them?
3 QUESTION 3:
According to the girl, why does the same line come back three times in the story?
4 QUESTION 4:
What does the girl mean when she says the story was “built to be heard”?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Listen for the moment the girl says why she thinks the story was told out loud. Listen for where she learned about the old way of telling stories. Listen for what she says about the line that comes back three times.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: W.8.3.b | MS ELA: Grade 8
